answer:
We take the Laplace transform of both sides. Using \( \mathcal{L}\{y'\} = sY(s) - y(0) \) and \( y(0)=0 \), we get  
\( sY(s) - Y(s) = \frac{1}{s+1} \), so \( Y(s) = \frac{1}{(s-1)(s+1)} \).  
Partial fractions give \( Y(s) = \frac{1/2}{s-1} - \frac{1/2}{s+1} \), and inverting yields \( y(x) = \frac{1}{2}e^x - \frac{1}{2}e^{-x} \).  

\boxed{F}
